title: Revert to  slirp4netns
body:
### Issue Description

Pasta is not fit for purpose: it fails when used in rootless containers (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22197 and https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22190) and breaks existing installations.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set up a quadlet that requires net.
2. Reboot.
3. See the quadlet randomly work or not.
4. When it doesn't, run `sudo journalctl` and see:
   


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

```pasta[...]: External interface not usable```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

I expect my containers to run every single time like it did before with slirp4netns.

How do you want to be taken seriously vs Docker when you push broken software?

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.0
API Version:  5.0.0
Go Version:   go1.22.0
Built:        Tue Mar 19 00:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

Fedora CoreOS, guys, not a 3rd party distro.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

It might be good to write tests too, so that changes like this one can be tested first, instead of waiting for your customers to yell when it fails.

comments.body:
> I expect my containers to run every single time like it did before with slirp4netns.

See https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/3312#issuecomment-901164986 for an issue equivalent to #22197 but with slirp4netns.

You can change the the default back in containers.conf as documented, we have no plans to switch back to slirp4netns as default.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22197 is a valid bug and as such still open but IMO no reason to revert the default back and the systemd issue contains possible work around.

So you mean to say that you pushed a change you knew would break everything out there and that if our machines were currently running podman, that we would have to redeploy them all with a non-default configuration, just to make them work at all? (don't mention using `sleep`)

You know you have competition right? How can I decently push for podman, when it's already in version 5 yet it has barely any more stability guarantee than a version 0.5? I really don't mean to be total ass about it but that's not how you win market share.

I did not consider that units would fail to start when they are started before the network is fully setup. I personally tested it beforehand and guess what it works perfectly fine in my env because networking is quick enough that it is already done before systemd starts the user session so I had no reason to believe otherwise.

And how do you expect us to know about problems that were never encountered by us. We knew pasta does things differently and has the chance to cause regressions for some users that is why we changed this in a major version 5.0, yes you must expect breaking changes there. And yes we try to make sure that minor and patch releases do not break users. We think pasta is a better fit long term as it offers more flexibility and the pasta maintainers, such as @sbrivio-rh, are on top of reported issues and a very quick to fix them so I see no reason to switch the default back because of one specific issue that is not directly under our control. 

> I really don't mean to be total ass about it

Then maybe figure out a way to communicate that in way so it does not sound like that.
